title: 4.2 Cell Division
description: Cell division: mitosis (prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase) and meiosis (Meiosis I and Meiosis II).

questions.question: What is the basic structural and functional unit of life?
What distinguishes living organisms from non-living organisms?
Which unicellular organism is given as an example that reproduces by cell division?
According to cell theory, from what do new cells arise?
The fundamental principle that new cells arise from pre-existing cells is known as what?
Who originally postulated the cell doctrine in 1858?
The cell doctrine provides the basis for what?
Cell division is a basic process in all living things where a parent cell divides into how many daughter cells?
Cells on the path to cell division proceed through a series of precisely timed and carefully regulated stages that ultimately produce what?
As a cell grows, what happens to the volume of cytoplasm relative to the cell membrane?
options: It becomes larger, resulting in low surface area to volume ratio
It becomes smaller, resulting in high surface area to volume ratio
questions.correctAnswer: It becomes larger, resulting in low surface area to volume ratio

questions.question: When the surface area to volume ratio is low, what becomes inadequate for the cell to survive?
options: Simple diffusion across the cell membrane
questions.correctAnswer: Simple diffusion across the cell membrane

questions.question: As the size of a cell increases, what is highly minimized?
options: The controlling power of the nucleus
questions.correctAnswer: The controlling power of the nucleus

questions.question: What mechanism does a cell employ to solve the problems of low surface area to volume ratio and minimized nuclear control?
In unicellular organisms, what is cell division a means of?
When a single-celled Amoeba divides, what has it increased?
options: Its population or number of offspring
questions.correctAnswer: Its population or number of offspring

questions.question: In multicellular organisms, cell division is a means of increasing cell number for what purposes?
options: Growth or to replace damaged or dead cells and repair the body
questions.correctAnswer: Growth or to replace damaged or dead cells and repair the body

questions.question: In sexually reproducing organisms, cell division occurs around reproductive organs as a means of getting what?
Cell division consists of which two subdivisions?
Nuclear division results in the separation and distribution of what to daughter cells?
options: Duplicated genetic materials of the mother cell
questions.correctAnswer: Duplicated genetic materials of the mother cell

questions.question: Nuclear division distributes genetic materials by which processes?
What is the separation of cytoplasmic components into daughter cells called?
What is mitosis?
options: A type of nuclear division where duplicated chromosomes are distributed between two identical daughter cells
A type of cell division that produces gametes
A process that reduces chromosome number by half
questions.correctAnswer: A type of nuclear division where duplicated chromosomes are distributed between two identical daughter cells

questions.question: In mitosis, a diploid (2n) mother cell gives rise to what kind of daughter cells?
Who first coined the term mitosis in 1882?
What did Walther Flemming discover about chromosomes during cell division?
options: They split longitudinally to distribute themselves equally between two daughter cells
questions.correctAnswer: They split longitudinally to distribute themselves equally between two daughter cells

questions.question: The end result of mitosis is growth of the eukaryotic organism and what else?
options: Replacement of damaged or dead cells
questions.correctAnswer: Replacement of damaged or dead cells

questions.question: After fertilization, the growth of the zygote occurs by cell division through mitosis into the 2-cell stage, then 4-cell stage, 8-cell stage, 16-cell stage, and so on. What is this process called?
Mitosis as nuclear division is divided into which series of phases?
options: Prophase I, metaphase I, anaphase I, telophase I

questions.question: What happens to the nuclear membrane during prophase?
What happens to the nucleolus during prophase?
During prophase, what happens to chromosomes?
options: They shorten, thicken, and become visible
questions.correctAnswer: They shorten, thicken, and become visible

questions.question: During prophase, each duplicated chromosome is composed of what?
options: Two sister chromatids containing identical genetic material
questions.correctAnswer: Two sister chromatids containing identical genetic material

questions.question: During prophase, what begins to move to opposite poles of the cell?
From where do spindle fibres emerge during prophase?
Centrosomes are located where in relation to the nucleus?
How many centrosomes are present in a cell during prophase?
What phase of mitosis is known as the arrangement phase?
During metaphase, mitotic spindles are fully developed with centrosomes at what location?
During metaphase, chromosomes line up end-to-end along what structure?
options: The metaphase plate or equatorial plane
questions.correctAnswer: The metaphase plate or equatorial plane

questions.question: During metaphase, each sister chromatid is attached to a spindle fibre originating from where?
What phase of mitosis is known as the migrating phase?
During anaphase, what proteins binding the sister chromatids together break down?
During anaphase, separated sister chromatids are pulled apart by which structure?
During anaphase, the mitotic spindle drags one chromatid to one pole and the other chromatid to the opposite pole. What does this ensure?
options: Daughter cells receive different numbers of chromosomes
Daughter cells receive chromosomes that are the same in number and kind
The cell divides into four daughter cells
The chromosome number is reduced by half
questions.correctAnswer: Daughter cells receive chromosomes that are the same in number and kind

questions.question: What phase of mitosis is known as a reverse of prophase?
During telophase, what reappears to surround each set of chromosomes?
What happens to the mitotic spindle during telophase?
When does cytokinesis take place?
options: After the four stages of mitosis are completed
questions.correctAnswer: After the four stages of mitosis are completed

questions.question: In animal cells, what enables the cytoplasm to pinch or constrict in the middle during cytokinesis?
In plant cells, what prevents the cytoplasm from simply pinching off during cytokinesis?
options: The hard cell wall in addition to the cell membrane
questions.correctAnswer: The hard cell wall in addition to the cell membrane

questions.question: In plant cells, what is laid down between the two daughter cells during cytokinesis?
What is the middle wall that joins two adjacent plant cells called?
The redistribution of duplicated chromosomes through mitosis produces two daughter cells that are identical in what?
After the union of haploid sperm and haploid egg, the resulting zygote is what?
How do human beings become made up of millions of cells starting from a single zygote?
options: By repeated cell division through mitosis
questions.correctAnswer: By repeated cell division through mitosis

questions.question: Why do cells need to be replaced continuously in the body?
options: Cells have a finite life span and wear out or become damaged
questions.correctAnswer: Cells have a finite life span and wear out or become damaged

questions.question: What is a spindle fiber?
options: A short carbohydrate fiber in the nucleus
A long protein fiber extending from centrioles
A lipid fiber in the cell membrane
A DNA strand in the chromosome
questions.correctAnswer: A long protein fiber extending from centrioles

questions.question: During which stage of cell division is the spindle fiber necessary to pull chromosomes towards the pole?
What are homologous chromosomes?
options: Two identical copies of the same chromosome
Couples of one maternal and one paternal chromosome paired up during fertilization
Chromosomes found only in sex cells
Chromosomes that have no genetic information
questions.correctAnswer: Couples of one maternal and one paternal chromosome paired up during fertilization

questions.question: Each homologous pair of chromosomes represents a parent for what type of character?
options: A unit character such as height, body colour, or sex
questions.correctAnswer: A unit character such as height, body colour, or sex

questions.question: In humans, each diploid body cell has how many pairs of homologous chromosomes?
What is the total number of chromosomes in a human diploid body cell?
What are sister chromatids?
options: Two copies of one maternal or paternal chromosome linked by cohesive protein on the centromere
questions.correctAnswer: Two copies of one maternal or paternal chromosome linked by cohesive protein on the centromere

questions.question: What links sister chromatids together?
Meiosis is a characteristic feature of what type of organisms?
In which organs does meiosis occur in female animals?
In which organs does meiosis occur in male animals?
In which parts of flowering plants does meiosis occur?
Meiosis involves how many fissions of the nucleus?
Meiosis gives rise to how many gametes or sex cells?
Each gamete produced by meiosis possesses what number of chromosomes compared to the mother cell?
What is meiosis called because the final daughter cells are haploid compared to the diploid mother cell?
During gametogenesis in human ovaries and testes, the 46 chromosomes in the initial mother cell are reduced by half to how many chromosomes by meiosis?
Human sperm and egg cells have how many chromosomes each?
When a sperm and egg join together at fertilization, what is the chromosome number of the resulting zygote?
If human gametes had 46 chromosomes like body cells, how many chromosomes would be in the zygote?
During prophase I of meiosis, what happens to the chromosomes?
options: They appear shortened and thickened and form two chromatids
questions.correctAnswer: They appear shortened and thickened and form two chromatids

questions.question: During prophase I, what do homologous chromosomes do?
During metaphase I, where do chromosomes align?
options: At the poles of the cell
On spindle fiber, lining up in the middle or metaphase plate
questions.correctAnswer: On spindle fiber, lining up in the middle or metaphase plate

questions.question: In metaphase I, does the centromere joining sister chromatids separate?
During anaphase I, what moves to the end or pole of the cell?
options: The pair of chromatids from each homologous pair
questions.correctAnswer: The pair of chromatids from each homologous pair

questions.question: During anaphase I, why does reduction to haploid (n) take place?
options: Because homologous chromosomes of male and female parents separate
questions.correctAnswer: Because homologous chromosomes of male and female parents separate

questions.question: What happens to the nuclear membrane during telophase I?
What happens to cells during telophase I?
options: They immediately enter prophase II without any changes
The cells begin to divide, with some undergoing full cytokinesis and others having prolonged interphase but no further DNA duplication
questions.correctAnswer: The cells begin to divide, with some undergoing full cytokinesis and others having prolonged interphase but no further DNA duplication

questions.question: During prophase II, what is formed?
During prophase II, where does the chromosome move?
options: To the poles of the cell
Toward the middle of the cell
questions.correctAnswer: Toward the middle of the cell

questions.question: During metaphase II, where do the chromosomes line up?
options: At the poles of each cell
On the metaphase plate with spindle attached to sister chromatids from opposite poles
questions.correctAnswer: On the metaphase plate with spindle attached to sister chromatids from opposite poles

questions.question: During anaphase II, what divides?
During anaphase II, what happens to sister chromatids?
options: They separate and are pulled to the opposite ends of each cell
questions.correctAnswer: They separate and are pulled to the opposite ends of each cell

questions.question: During telophase II, what reappears?
During telophase II, what happens to chromosomes?
options: They return to the interphase state
questions.correctAnswer: They return to the interphase state

questions.question: Telophase II results in how many daughter cells?
Each daughter cell at the end of telophase II has what chromosome number compared to the initial parent cell?
Which division of meiosis is similar to mitosis?
Mitosis is division of what type of cells?
Meiosis is division of what type of cells?
Meiosis occurs only in which organs?
In meiosis I, what separates and moves to opposite cells?
Meiosis I is a reduction by half, going from what to what?
In meiosis II, what separates and moves to opposite poles of the cell?
What happens in meiosis that does not happen in mitosis regarding the number of cell divisions?
Mitosis results in how many daughter cells?
In mitosis, the daughter cells have what chromosome number compared to the parent cell?
Prokaryotic organisms like Bacteria do not divide by what process?
